name: aws-iam-best-practices
description: IAM policy review, hardening, and least privilege implementation
capabilities: Review IAM policies for security issues and overly permissive access
Identify users and roles without MFA enabled
Detect old access keys requiring rotation
Create MFA-required and IP-restricted access policies
Provide IAM hardening checklists and automation scripts
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 48 static findings are false positives. The skill contains legitimate AWS CLI commands for IAM security auditing, AWS documentation URLs, RFC5737 documentation IP addresses, and standard IAM policy syntax. This is a defensive security skill focused on IAM hardening and least privilege implementation with no malicious functionality.
valueStatement: This skill helps Claude and Codex users implement AWS IAM best practices by reviewing policies, identifying overly permissive access, and generating least-privilege IAM configurations.
limitations: Does not execute AWS API calls - provides guidance and templates only
Cannot modify live IAM configurations directly
Requires manual implementation of recommended policies
Python automation script requires boto3 and AWS credentials

useCases.title: Security Engineer Auditing IAM
useCases.description: Security engineer reviews AWS account IAM configurations quarterly, identifying overly permissive policies and users without MFA for remediation

useCases.title: Developer Creating S3 Access Policy
useCases.description: Developer creates a least-privilege S3 access policy that grants only required permissions to specific bucket prefixes

useCases.title: DevOps Implementing MFA Enforcement
useCases.description: DevOps team implements MFA-required policies to enforce multi-factor authentication for sensitive AWS operations

promptTemplates.title: Basic IAM Policy Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my IAM policies for common security issues like overly permissive actions, wildcard resources, or missing conditions.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a quick security check of existing IAM configurations

promptTemplates.title: Find Users Without MFA
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all IAM users in my account that do not have MFA enabled and generate a list for remediation.
promptTemplates.scenario: When auditing MFA compliance across the account

promptTemplates.title: Create Least Privilege S3 Policy
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an IAM policy that allows read and write access to a specific S3 bucket prefix for a single user, using conditions for security.
promptTemplates.scenario: When granting minimal S3 permissions to a new user

promptTemplates.title: Generate MFA Enforcement Policy
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate an IAM policy that denies all actions unless the requester has authenticated with MFA.
promptTemplates.scenario: When hardening account security with MFA requirements

bestPractices: Use AWS managed policies instead of inline policies for better auditability and reusability
Implement least privilege by granting only the specific actions needed for each use case
Enable MFA for all users, especially those with administrative access
antiPatterns: Using wildcard (*) in Action or Resource elements - always specify exact permissions
Granting administrative access as a default - use role-based access control instead
Skipping regular access reviews - schedule quarterly IAM policy audits

faq.question: Does this skill execute AWS API calls?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance, templates, and example commands. It does not directly modify AWS IAM configurations.

faq.question: Can this skill create IAM policies for me?
faq.answer: Yes, it can generate IAM policy JSON templates based on your requirements for least privilege access.

faq.question: What AWS CLI commands does this skill reference?
faq.answer: It provides examples using standard AWS CLI commands like list-policies, get-policy, list-users, and simulate-principal-policy for IAM auditing.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use in production environments?
faq.answer: Yes, this is a defensive security skill focused on hardening IAM. It provides read-only analysis and template generation.

faq.question: Does this skill work with AWS Organizations?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on account-level IAM but can analyze policies across accounts when run with appropriate cross-account access.

faq.question: What Python libraries are needed for automation?
faq.answer: The included Python script requires boto3 and standard library modules. Configure AWS credentials before running.
